manufacturer.value: Wall's (Unilever / The Magnum Ice Cream Company)

ingredientsText.value: Reconstituted Skimmed Milk, Water, Glucose Syrup, Sugar, Coconut Fat, Fructose, Whey Solids (Milk), Stabilisers (Guar Gum, Tara Gum, Carrageenan, Locust Bean Gum), Emulsifier (Mono- and Di-Glycerides of Fatty Acids), Colours (Annatto Norbixin, Curcumin), Flavourings
ingredientsText.note: The fat is coconut (vegetable) fat, not cream or milk fat; the only dairy is reconstituted skimmed milk and whey solids supplying protein. A 2018 Which? investigation independently recorded no cream, fresh milk or vanilla. Open Food Facts lists a generic 'vegetable fat'; the 'coconut' specificity rests on the One Stop label.

sugars.note: 17g per 100g from the manufacturer archival snapshot (primary). The per-100ml figure of 7.5g is independently corroborated by One Stop; Open Food Facts records a higher 21g per 100g that appears stale or a different pack and is withheld pending re-verification (a measure mismatch the Phase 2 gate should auto-raise as a disagreement).

regulatoryStatus.value: Does not meet the compositional standard for 'dairy ice cream', which requires the fat to be milk-derived; its fat is coconut (vegetable) fat, so it is marketed as 'Soft Scoop Ice Cream Dessert'. In GB the 'ice cream' and 'dairy ice cream' compositional specifications now sit in a voluntary industry code (Ice Cream Alliance / Euroglace) rather than statute.
regulatoryStatus.note: Corrects a common misconception: current GB law does not bar a vegetable-fat product from the term 'ice cream'. The operative point is that coconut fat disqualifies it from 'dairy ice cream'. The revocation and voluntary-code position should be re-verified against the primary statutory instrument before this is treated as settled.

provenance.value: Reconstituted skimmed milk and whey solids declared on the current label
Carries a may-contain-soy precautionary statement on the current label
